content: Tolerance diagnosis based on: documented dose escalation without disease progression; diminished clinical effect despite adherence; withdrawal symptoms suggesting physical dependence (often accompanies tolerance). Differentiate from: disease progression (worsening condition requires higher doses); pseudoaddiction (undertreated pain seeking relief); hyperalgesia (opioid-induced increased pain sensitivity - paradoxical response requiring dose reduction not increase). Quantitative assessment: track dose-response relationship over time; calculate morphine milligram equivalents (MME) for opioids; monitor therapeutic indices. Experimental measures: receptor binding studies show decreased density/sensitivity; metabolic studies demonstrate increased clearance. Tolerance vs addiction: tolerance is expected physiologic adaptation; addiction involves compulsive use despite harm, craving, loss of control.
